file_insights.changes: Added 61-line cmd_clean function implementing cleanup logic with state filtering
file_insights.key_details: Public function signature: cmd_clean(root: &Path, dry_run: bool, older_than: Option<u64>, state: Option<String>) -> anyhow::Result<()>

file_insights.changes: Replaced 9-line placeholder with direct call to cards::cmd_clean(&root, dry_run, older_than, state)
file_insights.key_details: Removed debug println statements, wired Clean variant to actual implementation

patterns_discovered.description: Public cmd_* functions in cards module are called directly from main.rs match arms, enabling modular command handling
Optional state parameter accepts 'done', 'failed', 'both' with 'both'/None as default behavior
older_than uses Option<u64> with .unwrap_or(30) to set default 30-day threshold for failed cards
cmd_clean chains three reusable functions: scan_cards_for_cleanup → perform_cleanup → print_cleanup_summary

gotchas_discovered.description: Uses .to_string_lossy().contains("/done/") to filter paths - brittle and could match unintended directories
match arm Some("both") | None treats both identically without explicit documentation why default is 'both'
let valid_states = ["done", "failed", "both"] duplicates enum-like values that could be defined centrally

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully wired Clean command to full cmd_clean implementation replacing placeholder
approach_outcome.implementation_quality: Functional with proper error handling and state filtering, though some code could be more robust

recommendations.suggestion: Replace string-contains path filtering with path component comparison (e.g., path.components().any(|c| c.as_os_str() == "done"))
Extract valid_states to a const array or enum at module level for reusability across validation
Add doc comment explaining why older_than defaults to 30 days and what this threshold applies to
Add unit tests for state filtering logic, especially edge cases around path matching
